title: Make scrub feedback more immediate while dragging
summary: 0.6.50 makes scrub readback feel more responsive by reusing the player-owned presentation timeline for state and by refreshing the demo and plugin scrub surfaces during active scrub interactions.
changes: Added a shared presentation-state reader that prefers the player-owned master timeline during seek recovery instead of relying only on raw `VideoPlayer.time` for readback.
Scrub interactions now refresh the demo and plugin scrub surfaces immediately through the existing UI-readback seam instead of waiting for the slower rig refresh cadence.
Reduced the plugin scrub field display holdoff so attached scrub feedback catches up faster.
reasoning: The transport model was already working, but scrub still felt dull because state surfaces lagged behind the active seek target.
Reusing the existing player-owned timeline and UI-readback seams keeps this as a feedback slice instead of layering a second scrub subsystem on top.
operatorVisibleOutcomes: Dragging scrub should update demo3 and plugin scrub state more quickly.
Video scrub readback should feel more immediate after seeks because state follows the player-owned timeline earlier.
This slice does not change slideshow, image ordering, or right-stick volume behavior.
knownIssues: This slice does not add slideshow behavior.
Deferred gaze polish and right-stick volume remain separate queued slices.
